name: Encinas Reales, Benameji and Cuevas Bajas — circular from Cuevas de San Marcos
name_original: Encinas Reales, Benamejí y Cuevas Bajas — circular desde Cuevas de San Marcos
region_intro: Road cycling around Cuevas De San Marcos offers diverse terrain, characterized by the Sierra del Camorro mountain range, the Genil River, and the expansive Iznájar Reservoir. The landscape features geological formations from the Jurassic period, including limestone and sandstone outcrops, alongside fertile areas with olive and almond groves. This varied environment provides road cyclists with options ranging from challenging climbs in mountainous areas to scenic routes along river valleys and reservoir shores. Best road cycling routes around Cuevas De San Marcos The most popular road cycling route is Encinas Reales, Benameji and Cuevas Bajas — circular from Cuevas de San Marcos, a 28.0 miles (45.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 24 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores rural villages and agricultural landscapes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Mirador del Adarve – Iznájar Dam and Reservoir loop from Cuevas de San Marcos, a difficult 53.7 miles (86.4 km) path. This demanding loop offers significant climbs and panoramic views of the Iznájar Dam and Reservoir. Local road cyclists also love the Views of the Genil River – Iznájar Dam and Reservoir loop from Cuevas de San Marcos, a 48.5 miles (78.0 km) trail leading through varied terrain with views of the Genil River and the Iznájar Reservoir, often completed in about 4 hours 19 minutes. Road cycling around Cuevas De San Marcos is defined by the mountainous Sierra del Camorro, the winding Genil River, and the vast Iznájar Reservoir. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides through olive groves to difficult routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Cuevas De San Marcos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 6 reviews. More than 900 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Cuevas De San Marcos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Cuevas de San Marcos?
A: There are over 10 road cycling routes available around Cuevas de San Marcos on komoot, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. These routes explore the diverse landscapes of the region, from mountainous terrain to river valleys.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling in Cuevas de San Marcos?
A: Road cycling in Cuevas de San Marcos offers a mix of challenging and scenic terrain. You can expect routes that traverse the mountainous &lt;strong&gt;Sierra del Camorro&lt;/strong&gt;, wind alongside the &lt;strong&gt;Genil River&lt;/strong&gt;, and provide expansive views of the &lt;strong&gt;Iznájar Reservoir&lt;/strong&gt;. The landscape also features fertile areas with olive and almond groves, and geological formations from the Jurassic period, including limestone and sandstone outcrops.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Cuevas de San Marcos?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016900640/encinas-reales-benameji-and-cuevas-bajas-circular-from-cuevas-de-san-marcos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Encinas Reales, Benameji and Cuevas Bajas — circular from Cuevas de San Marcos&lt;/a&gt; route is a moderate 28-mile loop exploring rural villages and agricultural areas. Another option is the demanding &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20123071" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Adarve – Iznájar Dam and Reservoir loop from Cuevas de San Marcos&lt;/a&gt;, which offers significant climbs and panoramic views.

Q: What natural landmarks can I see while cycling in Cuevas de San Marcos?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can enjoy views of the vast &lt;strong&gt;Iznájar Reservoir&lt;/strong&gt;, the largest in Andalucía, and the dramatic &lt;strong&gt;Sierra del Camorro&lt;/strong&gt; mountain range. Routes often follow the course of the &lt;strong&gt;Genil River&lt;/strong&gt;. For specific viewpoints, consider visiting &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1853196" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Cedrón Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, which offers stunning vistas, or exploring the area around &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5316830" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Cañuelo Trail – Iznájar Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sites accessible by road bike?
A: Yes, beyond the natural beauty, you can incorporate cultural and historical points of interest into your rides. The area around the Iznájar Reservoir features landmarks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/884528" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iznájar Castle and the Parish Church of Saint James the Apostle&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter the ancient medieval city of Medina Belda in Sierra Camorro, or the charming 18th-century waterwheel, Noria De La Aceña, which are part of the region's rich heritage.

Q: Are there routes with panoramic views of the Iznájar Reservoir?
A: Absolutely. The mountainous terrain, particularly around the Sierra del Camorro, provides excellent opportunities for panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20123071" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Adarve – Iznájar Dam and Reservoir loop from Cuevas de San Marcos&lt;/a&gt; is specifically known for its demanding climbs and rewarding vistas of the Iznájar Dam and Reservoir. Another route, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33691352" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Genil River – Iznájar Dam and Reservoir loop from Cuevas de San Marcos&lt;/a&gt;, also offers scenic views of the reservoir.

Q: What is the overall difficulty level of road cycling in Cuevas de San Marcos?
A: Road cycling in Cuevas de San Marcos caters to various skill levels, though it leans towards more challenging rides. Out of 13 available routes, 5 are rated as moderate, and 8 are considered difficult, featuring significant elevation gains. This makes it an ideal destination for cyclists seeking rewarding climbs and descents.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: While many routes in Cuevas de San Marcos feature significant climbs and are rated moderate to difficult, some sections through olive groves and along flatter river valleys might be more accessible. For a moderate experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016900640/encinas-reales-benameji-and-cuevas-bajas-circular-from-cuevas-de-san-marcos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Encinas Reales, Benameji and Cuevas Bajas — circular from Cuevas de San Marcos&lt;/a&gt; route, which explores rural villages and agricultural landscapes. Always check the route profile for elevation and distance to ensure it matches your group's ability.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Cuevas de San Marcos?
A: The road cycling routes in Cuevas de San Marcos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 6 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the challenging climbs, and the stunning views of the Iznájar Reservoir and surrounding natural landscapes.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Cuevas de San Marcos?
A: While specific parking areas for cyclists are not detailed, Cuevas de San Marcos is a small municipality. You can typically find public parking within the town center or near the starting points of many routes. It's advisable to check local signage for parking regulations upon arrival.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Cuevas de San Marcos?
A: Andalucía generally offers favorable conditions for cycling for much of the year. Spring (March to May) and Autumn (September to November) are often considered ideal, with pleasant temperatures and less extreme weather. Summers can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon rides preferable, while winters are generally mild but can have cooler temperatures and occasional rain.
tips: A good viewpoint of the Iznájar reservoir from the dam on the Genil River that feeds it. It is the largest reservoir in Andalusia. Its construction finished in 1969 and lasted a decade.

A beautiful town that is the beginning of the 9. stage of the Camino de Santiago - Camino Mozárabe de Málaga. Near the Ermita del Calvario there is a free hostel for pilgrims. The keys are found at the local police office.

One of the towns visited by King Felipe V in the 18th century as part of a program to learn about the country. Among the places of interest are: the Parish of the Immaculate Conception, the Hermitage of San Sebastián and the Museum of the Duchess of Benamejí (a historical tour of nineteenth-century romantic banditry).
